title: P1 fix: manifest_changed must never reshape a data command's JSON envelope
action: P1 fix (review-phase-1.md, api-contract reviewer): emit() currently nests a data command's real result under {manifest_changed, manifest_changed_hint, result} when drift is detected, breaking any consumer parsing the steady-state JSON shape. Fix: never alter stdout's top-level shape for data commands. Move the drift signal off the data path - print the hint to stderr only when drift is detected, and leave stdout's JSON shape (the bare result) unchanged in every case. --help --json may still report a manifest_changed field in its own envelope (that command's shape already varies by design).

truths: bee <cmd> --json stdout has an identical top-level JSON shape whether or not drift is detected, for every data command
A drift notice, when present, appears only on stderr or only in --help --json output, never altering a data command's stdout shape

artifacts.substantive: emit() no longer wraps data-command stdout on drift

key_links: existing manifest_changed tests updated to assert stdout shape stability + stderr/help-only hint location
prohibitions: No change to the drift-detection logic itself (checkManifestDrift's hash comparison) - only where/how the signal is surfaced

trace.outcome: emit() no longer wraps a data command's stdout on drift; the manifest_changed hint now goes to stderr only, keeping stdout byte-stable
